title: OWASP Web Security Testing Guide v4.2

controls.title: Conduct Search Engine Discovery and Reconnaissance
controls.text: Use search engines and public sources to discover sensitive information about the target: exposed files, directories, credentials, configuration data, and internal paths indexed by search engines.
controls.evidence_expectation: Google/Shodan/Censys dork results; cached pages; exposed files list
controls.assessment_procedure: Run targeted dorks for the domain; check Shodan/Censys for exposed services; document all discovered sensitive data

controls.title: Fingerprint Web Server and Application
controls.text: Identify the web server software, version, operating system, and application framework to understand the attack surface and applicable CVEs.
controls.evidence_expectation: Nmap/Wappalyzer output; HTTP response headers; version banners
controls.assessment_procedure: Analyse HTTP response headers, error pages, and URL patterns; run technology detection tools; correlate versions with CVE databases

controls.title: Test Network and Application Configuration
controls.text: Identify misconfigured services, insecure HTTP methods, unnecessary services, default credentials, and debug endpoints that increase the attack surface.
controls.evidence_expectation: Service enumeration output; HTTP OPTIONS response; admin/debug endpoint discovery results
controls.assessment_procedure: Test HTTP methods allowed; check for exposed admin interfaces; verify no default credentials; enumerate all services

controls.title: Test HTTP Strict Transport Security and Security Headers
controls.text: Verify that HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy headers are correctly configured to mitigate a range of client-side attacks.
controls.evidence_expectation: Security headers report (SecurityHeaders.com or manual); curl response header dump
controls.assessment_procedure: Check all security headers present and correctly configured; verify HSTS max-age sufficient; validate CSP does not allow unsafe-inline or unsafe-eval

controls.title: Test Credentials Transported over Encrypted Channel
controls.text: Ensure all authentication credentials are submitted exclusively over HTTPS/TLS and that no credential leakage occurs in URLs, logs, or referer headers.
controls.evidence_expectation: Network capture showing TLS-encrypted credentials; absence of credentials in GET parameters
controls.assessment_procedure: Intercept login requests; verify TLS; check that passwords do not appear in URLs, browser history, or server logs

controls.title: Test for Bypassing Authentication Schema
controls.text: Test whether authentication controls can be bypassed through parameter manipulation, forced browsing, SQL injection, session token prediction, or logic flaws.
controls.evidence_expectation: Authentication bypass proof-of-concept; forced browse results; parameter manipulation test cases
controls.assessment_procedure: Attempt forced browsing to authenticated pages; test parameter manipulation; try SQL injection in login; test remember-me and stay-logged-in mechanisms

controls.title: Test Directory Traversal and File Include
controls.text: Test for path traversal vulnerabilities that allow access to files outside the web root, and Local/Remote File Inclusion flaws that enable execution of arbitrary code.
controls.evidence_expectation: Traversal test payloads and responses; successful file read proof-of-concept
controls.assessment_procedure: Test file parameters with traversal sequences; test URL includes; check for /etc/passwd, windows/win.ini reads; test with encoding variants

controls.title: Test for Reflected Cross-Site Scripting
controls.text: Test for reflected XSS in all user-controlled inputs including URL parameters, form fields, HTTP headers, and JSON/XML data submitted to the application.
controls.evidence_expectation: XSS payload execution screenshots; affected parameter list; browser console proof
controls.assessment_procedure: Test all input vectors with context-appropriate XSS payloads; verify reflected in response; test in different browsers; check for DOM XSS

controls.title: Test for SQL Injection
controls.text: Test all user-supplied inputs for SQL injection vulnerabilities including classic, blind (time-based and boolean-based), out-of-band, and second-order injection.
controls.evidence_expectation: SQLMap output or manual injection proof; database version/table data extracted; affected parameter list
controls.assessment_procedure: Test inputs with SQL metacharacters; run SQLMap against all entry points; test for blind injection; verify input not sanitised; check ORM usage

controls.title: Test Session Management Schema
controls.text: Analyse session token complexity, randomness, predictability, and proper secure/httponly flag configuration. Test for session fixation, hijacking, and improper invalidation.
controls.evidence_expectation: Session token analysis report (Burp Sequencer); token entropy calculation; flag inspection results
controls.assessment_procedure: Capture multiple session tokens; analyse entropy; check secure/httponly flags; test token reuse after logout; verify invalidation on password change

controls.title: Test Business Logic Flows
controls.text: Test for business logic vulnerabilities including workflow bypass, negative value manipulation, forced browsing through multi-step processes, and race conditions in critical operations.
controls.evidence_expectation: Business logic bypass test cases; race condition proof-of-concept; workflow manipulation evidence
controls.assessment_procedure: Map all multi-step workflows; test step skipping; try negative quantities; test concurrent requests for race conditions; test parameter manipulation in purchase flows

controls.title: Test for DOM-Based Cross-Site Scripting
controls.text: Identify DOM-based XSS by analysing client-side JavaScript that writes to dangerous sinks (innerHTML, document.write, eval) using attacker-controlled sources (location.hash, URL parameters).
controls.evidence_expectation: Source-sink analysis results; DOM XSS payload execution proof; affected JavaScript file and function
controls.assessment_procedure: Review JavaScript for dangerous sinks; trace data from URL sources to sinks; test payloads in hash/fragment parameters; use DOM Invader or manual analysis
